title: FBLA (Future Business Leaders of America)
html:
FBLA (Future Business Leaders of America)

The high-school business club where Yahir got his earliest pitching reps. FBLA appears across the late-2019 / early-2020 Grants Pass teen era notes as a recurring administrative line — never narrated, but consistently present, the kind of background commitment that anchors the whole senior-year cluster. It is one of the formative-org pieces (alongside YEBW and the Boys & Girls Club) that built Yahir's early founder voice — the elevator-pitch instinct, the contact-keeping discipline, the fear of not doing reframe.

The earliest mention is in 2019 11 13 coding zeal (November 2019) — "FBLA" sits in a sprawling Hamilton/Mecha/FBA/Costco mega-list alongside Chinese class recordings, Brenda talks, and the "Improving the human condition — what is the human condition?" line. By 2019 12 12 talk to brenda FBLA is more granular: "FBLA printing" — the kind of detail that suggests Yahir was running material logistics for the chapter, not just attending. The note pairs FBLA with APUSH review, wrestling, and the Jordan Cove fossil-fuels mini-thesis — the typical late-2019 stack of high school plus organized-business commitments plus political-economic systems thinking.

By the late-March 2020 brain dump (2020 03 30 facebook pandemic watchlist) the language shifts: "fbla shit intact / finish signing up for FBA" — note that the same line collapses FBLA (the high school org) and FBA (Amazon Fulfillment-By-Amazon, the hustle he was prepping with mom). The acronym overlap is itself the lineage: the same kid is doing organized business club and Amazon-arbitrage micro-business in the same paragraph, and they are functionally the same skill.

What FBLA matters for in the graph is the early pitching-reps story. The corpus shows pitch questions and elevator-pitch templates appearing as early as 2019 08 12 keep things simple ("It is a two way road — investors and invented. Dating." / "Have an elevator pitch for your self." / "Fear of not doing is greater than fear of doing it."). Those weren't theoretical; FBLA gave him the venue to actually deliver pitches at competitions. By the time he's drafting Rankett meetup-prep guides in 2026, the pitch-as-dating analogy and the contact-keeping discipline are in their tenth year of practice. FBLA is where the muscle started.

Source summaries

  • 2019 11 13 coding zeal
  • 2019 12 12 talk to brenda
  • 2020 03 30 facebook pandemic watchlist
